key_insight: DeepJEB++ scales small 2D multi-view seeds into a large, simulation-labeled 3D engineering dataset by augmenting in latent image space first and then lifting to geometry and labels.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster creation of manufacturable, physics-annotated 3D parts for solid mechanics design studies when manual CAD generation and simulation labeling are too costly.
method: Fine-tune a pretrained 2D latent diffusion model on multi-view renders and generate new views via latent interpolation.
Use a vision-language model filter to keep only manufacturable, plausible designs.
Lift validated images to 3D meshes with a domain-adapted generative foundation model, then auto-assign FE labels from recognized load and bolt interfaces.
evidence_signal: Starting from fewer than 400 seed designs, the pipeline produced 15,360 simulation-labeled 3D brackets, a 40x expansion, while evaluating manufacturability, label fidelity against SimJEB, and distributional consistency.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on accurate interface recognition and domain adaptation across stages, so errors in view synthesis, 3D lifting, or boundary-condition assignment could propagate into the final labels.
paper_title: DeepJEB++: Foundation Model-Driven Large-Scale 3D Engineering Dataset via 2D Latent Space Augmentation
